items.title: Binary and multi-class classification
items.description: Logistic regression and neural classifiers output Bernoulli or Categorical probabilities via sigmoid/softmax; training minimizes cross-entropy, equivalent to maximizing Bernoulli/Categorical likelihood.

items.title: Count and rate modeling
items.description: Poisson and Binomial models support click counts, conversion counts, and event rates; Poisson regression predicts λ from covariates for forecasting demand or failures.

items.title: Generative classification
items.description: Gaussian Naive Bayes and mixture models use Normal PDFs (and sometimes Categorical priors) to estimate class-conditional densities and posteriors.

items.title: Regression and uncertainty
items.description: Assuming Gaussian noise turns squared-error regression into Gaussian MLE; extensions use heteroscedastic or heavy-tailed models when Normal errors are inadequate.

items.title: Deep generative models
items.description: VAEs use Gaussian latents; diffusion models forward-process data with Gaussian noise—both rely heavily on Normal sampling and density formulas.
